stem: A ecology study surveys 20 sampled plots and observes 10 positive outcomes out of 20 (50.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (13 observations, rate = 46.2%) and Group B (7 observations, rate = 57.1%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 40% and 59% of the population. Estimate the true species occurrence rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.5. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.532 (range [0.524, 0.5392]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2993, 0.7007]. Point estimate: 0.532.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.49: 0.49×0.491 + 0.51×0.5714 = 0.532.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
